title: Sour Fluffy Low Carb Ginger Stir-fry
description: A lazy Sunday Vietnamese dish featuring a blend of sake and ginger for added flavor and moisture, perfect for low-carb eating.

ingredients.name: 1/2 cup low-carb friendly tofu or meat substitute
ingredients.notes: such as tempeh, chicken tender, etc.

instructions: Heat a wok or large frying pan over medium-high heat. Add oil and then grated ginger.
Add minced garlic and onion to the pan; stir-fry for about 1 minute until fragrant and soft.
Toss in the chopped bell pepper and cook until softened, around 2 minutes more.
Next, add low-carb friendly tofu or meat substitute into the pan. Stir fry for another 1-2 minutes.
Pour in sake, lime juice, soy sauce, and water. Stir to combine all ingredients evenly and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to medium-low, cover with a lid, and let simmer gently for about 5 minutes or until most of the liquid has evaporated.
Finally, add green onions and stir in until thoroughly combined. Cook for an additional minute if needed.
Serve hot as a side dish or main course.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
